summary: electron-orbit ships env-var harvester with obfuscated custom-TLS exfiltration channel
details: electron-orbit@1.0.15 is a credential-harvesting package masquerading as an archived OrbitDB/Electron toolkit. Its declared postinstall script (install.js) enumerates the installer's full process.env (accessed via an XOR-decoded string decoder to hide the 'process'/'env' identifiers from static scanners), filters out PATH-like keys, and writes the JSON dump to a marker file in os.tmpdir(). The declared main (dist/index.js) reads that marker back on require, XORs the contents with the key 'electron-orbit', and hex-encodes them for transport. The same module ships a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client (ClientHello, HKDF, AEAD in an internal 'aetherls' module) that opens raw net.connect(443, host) sockets to send hand-crafted HTTP GET requests — bypassing the https module, proxy inspection, and NODE_OPTIONS instrumentation. Node net API identifiers and the HTTP request bytes are all XOR-obfuscated string arrays. The outbound host configuration is gated at load time on sha256(process.env.BuildType) matching a hardcoded hex digest, a canonical target-selection pattern that activates the payload only on attacker-chosen victim environments (e.g., CI runners with a specific env var set). The package.json author 'OrbitDB Archive' and homepage pointing at github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron are cover-story metadata; the shipped code contains no OrbitDB, libp2p, IPC, or BrowserWindow functionality — only the env-dump and covert transport.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
